items.name: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited
items.headline: Diana extends $1.41B financing for GNK tender offer at $27.34/share
whatChanged: Diana Shipping extended its fully committed $1.412B acquisition financing (Tranche A $1.102B unchanged; Tranche B adjusted from $331M to $310M after Genco sold two vessels, Picardy and Predator)
Offer remains $27.34/share — $24.80 cash plus one Diana share valued at $2.54 (30-day VWAP as of June 16, 2026) — a 53% premium to Genco's undisturbed price and 6% premium to NAV
Diana reports growing shareholder tender support and reiterates eagerness to negotiate with Genco's Board, which has not yet engaged
Tender offer commenced May 4, 2026 at $23.50 cash, raised May 27, 2026 to $24.80 cash; stock component added to reach $27.34 implied value
items.decisionTakeaway: Diana's financing extension and reported growing tender support increase pressure on Genco's Board to engage, raising the probability that the $27.34/share unsolicited bid progresses toward a negotiated deal.

items.name: BlackRock, Inc.
items.headline: BlackRock registers 12M shares for HPS acquisition consideration redemption
whatChanged: BlackRock filed a prospectus supplement registering up to 12,035,866 shares of common stock issuable upon redemption of SubCo Units of BlackRock Saturn Subco, LLC
7,606,927 shares relate to closing-date consideration for the HPS Investment Partners acquisition (closed July 1, 2025)
4,428,939 shares relate to deferred consideration units contingent on post-closing conditions and financial performance milestones
SubCo Units become redeemable for common stock (or cash at Subco's option) following the one-year anniversary of closing; BlackRock expects to effect Direct Exchanges for stock
items.decisionTakeaway: This filing registers the share-issuance mechanics for the HPS deal's stock consideration, meaning up to ~12M BLK shares could enter the market as SubCo unit holders redeem beginning July 2026, with a contingent deferred tranche tied to performance milestones.

items.name: Devon Energy Corporation
items.headline: Devon 2026 Annual Meeting: All Directors Elected, KPMG Ratified, Say-on-Pay Approved
whatChanged: All 11 board nominees elected to one-year terms; Kelt Kindick received the most votes withheld (73.4M) among directors

Advisory say-on-pay approved with 842.1M votes for vs 45.3M against
Record date shares outstanding: 1,153,403,107
items.decisionTakeaway: Annual meeting produced no contested outcomes or governance surprises, confirming board continuity and shareholder support for auditor and executive compensation at Devon.

items.name: Nuvation Bio Inc.
items.headline: Nuvation Bio prices upsized $250M convertible notes due 2032
whatChanged: Priced $250.0M of 0.75% Convertible Senior Notes due 2032, upsized from previously announced $200.0M
Net proceeds estimated at ~$241.2M (~$277.6M if $37.5M over-allotment exercised); proceeds to fund capped call costs, repay senior secured loan in full, and general corporate purposes
Initial conversion price ~$7.84/share, a 35.0% premium to the June 25, 2026 closing price; capped call cap price set at $10.4580 (80.0% premium)
Notes not redeemable before July 6, 2029; mature July 1, 2032 unless earlier converted, redeemed, or repurchased
Entered into capped call transactions concurrently to reduce potential dilution upon conversion of Notes
items.decisionTakeaway: Nuvation Bio raised 12.5% of its market cap via a low-coupon convertible offering, using proceeds to retire its senior secured loan and fund operations, which extends runway but introduces equity dilution risk at a $7.84 conversion price.

items.name: Rocket Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
items.headline: Rocket Pharmaceuticals appoints new Principal Accounting Officer
whatChanged: John Militello resigning as VP of Finance, Senior Controller, Treasurer and Principal Accounting Officer effective July 7, 2026
Joseph Manhede appointed Principal Accounting Officer effective July 8, 2026
Manhede joined Rocket as Associate VP, Corporate Controller in April 2026; no new compensatory arrangements announced
items.decisionTakeaway: A routine, non-forced transition of the principal accounting officer with an internal successor already in place signals no disruption to financial reporting or controls.

items.name: Janus Henderson Group plc
items.headline: Janus Henderson completes $52/share take-private with Trian and General Catalyst
whatChanged: Take-private transaction with Trian, General Catalyst, and QIA has closed; shares not already owned by Trian converted to $52.00/share cash right
Ordinary shares delisted from NYSE; Janus Henderson now a privately held company

items.decisionTakeaway: The previously announced $52.00/share take-private has closed as expected, with JHG shares now delisted from the NYSE and the company operating as a private entity under Trian, General Catalyst, and QIA ownership.

items.name: Patrick Industries, Inc.
items.headline: Patrick Industries Announces All-Stock Merger with LCI Industries
whatChanged: Patrick Industries (PATK) and LCI Industries (LCI/Lippert Components) announced a proposed all-stock merger on June 30, 2026.
The transaction structure is all-stock; no exchange ratio, deal value, or ownership split is disclosed in this filing.
An S-4 registration statement including a joint proxy statement/prospectus will be filed with the SEC; the deal requires stockholder approvals from both companies.
CEO Andy Nemeth communicated the announcement to employees via email, with a webcast/conference call held the same morning.
The filing lists standard forward-looking-statement risk factors including integration risk, regulatory approval risk, and stockholder approval risk.
items.decisionTakeaway: Patrick Industries is merging with LCI Industries in an all-stock transaction combining two major RV/marine components suppliers, with material terms (exchange ratio, valuation, synergies) yet to be disclosed in subsequent S-4 filings.

items.name: Archer Aviation Inc.

Advisory say-on-pay approved (225.2M for vs 49.4M against)
items.decisionTakeaway: Shareholders rejected Archer's proposed redomestication from Delaware to Texas, leaving its corporate domicile unchanged and removing any governance or litigation-venue implications of the move.

items.name: Valvoline Inc.
items.headline: Valvoline Refinances Term B Loans at Lower Rate
whatChanged: Valvoline executed Amendment No. 1 to its Second Amended and Restated Credit Agreement (dated Dec. 1, 2025), refinancing all outstanding Initial Term B Loans with new Amendment No. 1 Term B Loans totaling $738.15M in commitments.
The Applicable Rate on Term B Loans was reduced 25 bps: from 2.00% to 1.75% for Term SOFR Loans and from 1.00% to 0.75% for Base Rate Loans.
Refinancing structure combines cashless rollover conversions by existing lenders ($690.27M, 93.51%) with new cash-funded loans led by Bank of Nova Scotia ($47.88M, 6.49%).
Amendment effective June 30, 2026; proceeds from new cash loans used to repay non-converting Initial Term B Loans, with all accrued interest paid on the effective date.
items.decisionTakeaway: Valvoline repriced its $738M Term B facility 25 bps lower, modestly reducing interest expense and improving debt-service headroom without changing total borrowing capacity.

items.name: Catalyst Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
items.headline: Catalyst reports positive Phase 1 vamorolone data showing no significant immunosuppression at clinical doses
whatChanged: Phase 1 study of vamorolone in healthy adults demonstrated glucocorticoid and anti-inflammatory activity without evidence of significant immunosuppression at clinical doses

Part A equipotency assessment showed comparable cortisol suppression between vamorolone (300 mg) and deflazacort (0.9 mg/kg), supporting current labeled DMD dosing and no special dosing considerations when switching from deflazacort
Part B ascending-dose evaluation found clinically relevant immunosuppressive effects only at the highest dose (40 mg/kg/day), with no relevant immunosuppression at 9 or 27 mg/kg/day
Data suggest vamorolone's favorable profile may support potential expansion into a broad range of chronic inflammatory rare diseases beyond DMD
items.decisionTakeaway: Positive Phase 1 data reinforce vamorolone's differentiated corticosteroid profile and support potential label expansion into broader chronic inflammatory rare disease indications, though the readout is early-stage in healthy volunteers.

keyMetrics.Part A doses: vamorolone 300 mg vs deflazacort 0.9 mg/kg (single dose)
keyMetrics.Part B doses: 9, 27, or 40 mg/kg once daily for 7 days

items.name: LTC Properties, Inc.

items.decisionTakeaway: LTC materially expanded its revolving credit capacity to $900M within a $1.1B total facility, providing significant incremental liquidity for a REIT with a ~$2B market cap.

keyMetrics.priorTermLoansFunded: $200,000,000 total ($50M 2028 + $55M 2029 + $55M 2030 + $40M 2032, all funded on First Amendment Effective Date, commitments now $0)

items.name: Cloudflare, Inc.

keyMetrics.maxSharesIssuableUnderPlan: 58,670,000 shares (plus up to 74,653,906 recycled from 2010 Plan)
keyMetrics.FY2027AnnualReserveIncreaseCap: 5% of outstanding shares (vs prior lesser-of 29,335,000 / 5% / Admin-determined)

items.name: REalloys Inc.

items.name: Patrick Industries, Inc.
items.headline: Patrick Industries to merge with LCI Industries in all-stock deal

items.name: Westrock Coffee Company

items.name: Seadrill Limited

items.name: Vistra Corp.

items.name: Papa John's International, Inc.

items.name: Fox Corporation
items.headline: Fox secures $1.0B term loan to finance Roku acquisition

items.name: Fox Corporation
items.headline: Fox Secures $1B Term Loan to Finance Roku Acquisition
whatChanged: Fox Corporation entered into a $1.0 billion Term Loan Credit Agreement dated June 30, 2026, with Morgan Stanley as Administrative Agent and a syndicate including Citi, Deutsche Bank, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan as Joint Lead Arrangers

The term loan is expressly tied to financing the Acquisition of Roku, Inc. pursuant to the merger agreement dated June 14, 2026, and will be used alongside an $11.0 billion Tranche A bridge facility (under a $12.0 billion bridge commitment letter)

Maturity is the second anniversary of the Closing Date; interest is Term SOFR or Base Rate with applicable margins ranging from 0.875%–1.375% (Term SOFR) and 0.000%–0.375% (Base Rate) based on Fox's public debt rating

The agreement includes a financial covenant (Operating Income Leverage Ratio under Section 5.03) and standard affirmative/negative covenants and events of default for an acquisition-financing term loan

Commitments terminate on the earliest of: 5 business days after the Acquisition Agreement termination date (extendible to March 14, 2028), consummation of the Acquisition without the term loans, or Fox's election to terminate
items.decisionTakeaway: Fox has put in place a $1.0B term loan and referenced an $11.0B bridge facility to fund its acquisition of Roku, signaling the debt-financing architecture for a transformative deal that materially increases leverage relative to Fox's current capital structure.

items.name: Horizon Bancorp, Inc.
items.headline: Jury verdict hits Horizon Bank with ~$3M Q2 charge
whatChanged: Jury returned civil verdict against subsidiary Horizon Bank in a 2018 lawsuit over repossession/credit reporting of a single vehicle from legacy indirect auto business
Total damages approaching $3.0M, including $2.5M punitive damages; Company plans to appeal
Company will add ~$3.0M pre-tax expense to existing reserve in Q2 2026 pending appeal
Indirect auto originations discontinued in 2023; Company states no broader exposure and no disruption to core community bank model
items.decisionTakeaway: A ~$3M pre-tax Q2 charge from a legacy auto-finance jury verdict is immaterial to a ~$1B market-cap bank and is being appealed, with management affirming no broader exposure.

items.name: W. P. Carey Inc.
items.headline: WPC Prices $350M 5.20% Senior Notes Due 2036
whatChanged: Priced $350M of 5.200% Senior Notes due 2036 at 99.015% of principal; expected settlement July 2, 2026.
Net proceeds to repay $350M of 4.250% Senior Notes maturing October 2026 and for general corporate purposes including future investments and revolver repayment.
Coupon steps up from 4.250% to 5.200% while extending maturity by approximately 10 years.
No change to AFFO guidance or operational disclosures; this is a standalone debt refinancing announcement.
items.decisionTakeaway: WPC is refinancing its October 2026 maturity into a new 10-year note at a higher 5.20% coupon, extending duration but increasing interest cost on $350M of debt.

items.name: Snowflake Inc.
items.headline: Snowflake Annual Meeting: Say-on-Pay Fails, Majority-Vote Proposal Approved
whatChanged: Stockholders rejected the non-binding advisory say-on-pay proposal: 124.5M shares Against vs 96.3M For, a significant governance signal on executive compensation.
A non-binding stockholder proposal requesting majority voting for director elections was approved: 143.1M For vs 77.4M Against.
Three Class III directors (Teresa Briggs, Mark McLaughlin, Sridhar Ramaswamy) elected to serve until the 2029 annual meeting; McLaughlin received notable 89.3M withheld votes.
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP ratified as independent auditor for fiscal year ending January 31, 2027 (287.9M For).
items.decisionTakeaway: The failed say-on-pay vote and approved majority-voting proposal signal mounting shareholder pressure on Snowflake's executive compensation and governance practices, which could drive board engagement on pay structure changes ahead.

items.name: Talos Energy Inc.
items.headline: Talos to acquire Shell Gulf deepwater assets for ~$450-500M net
whatChanged: Talos signed definitive agreement to jointly acquire Shell's Gulf of America deepwater assets (Coulomb field 50% WI/operated; Na Kika 25% non-operated WI) with Ridgewood Energy
Net cash consideration expected at ~$450–500M after interim cash flow from July 1, 2025 effective date; $42.5M deposit already in escrow
Acquired assets add ~23 MMBoe proved reserves, 10 MMBoe probable reserves, and 16 MBoe/d Q1 2026 production (~77% oil)
Borrowing base increased $150M to $850M via incremental lender commitments; transaction funded with cash on hand and debt
BP holds 30-day preferential right on Na Kika interests; close expected by end of 2026; 2026 guidance to be updated post-close
items.decisionTakeaway: Talos is acquiring high-margin, oil-weighted Gulf deepwater assets at ~$450–500M net (roughly 20–23% of market cap) with lender-backed financing, adding meaningful reserves and production while maintaining leverage targets.

keyMetrics.netCashConsideration: $450–500M (net to Talos, after interim cash flow; gross $850M)
keyMetrics.provedReservesAcquired: ~23 MMBoe (NSAI SEC YE2025, net to Talos)

items.name: NCR Atleos Corporation
items.headline: NCR Atleos stockholders overwhelmingly approve Brink's acquisition
whatChanged: Both Brink's shareholders and NCR Atleos stockholders voted overwhelmingly to approve the previously announced acquisition of NCR Atleos by Brink's at special meetings held June 30, 2026.
The transaction has received Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust Improvements Act clearance.
Closing is expected by end of Q1 2027, subject to remaining regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.
Prior comparable 8-K (June 18) disclosed two stockholder lawsuits and demand letters challenging the merger; this filing does not reference those matters.
items.decisionTakeaway: Shareholder approval and HSR clearance remove two key deal-closing conditions for the Brink's–NCR Atleos merger, leaving remaining regulatory approvals as the primary gating item before an expected Q1 2027 close.

items.name: LCI Industries
items.headline: LCI Industries announces merger with Patrick Industries
whatChanged: LCI Industries and Patrick Industries have entered into a proposed merger transaction, with Patrick to file an S-4 registration statement including a joint proxy statement/prospectus.
The transaction will involve issuance of Patrick common stock to LCI stockholders as part of the consideration structure.
LCI filed this communication under Rule 425/Rule 14a-12, soliciting proxies from both LCI and Patrick stockholders for approval of the transaction.

The filing identifies directors and executive officers of both companies as participants in the proxy solicitation, with detailed beneficial ownership changes disclosed through Form 3/4 filings dating from March through June 2026.
The filing references a combination investor presentation containing non-GAAP financial measures and reconciliations related to the anticipated strategic and financial benefits of the transaction.
items.decisionTakeaway: LCI Industries is merging with Patrick Industries in a stock-for-stock transaction that consolidates two major RV/aftermarket components suppliers, with stockholder approval and regulatory clearances still required to close.

items.name: Disc Medicine, Inc.
items.headline: Disc Medicine amends Hercules loan, draws $30M, extends tranches
whatChanged: Amended Hercules Capital loan agreement to draw down $30M of Tranche 1-B Advance immediately
Split existing $50M Tranche 1-C into $25M available through March 31, 2027 and $25M Tranche 1-D available through April 30, 2027
Extended Tranche 2 drawdown period to December 15, 2027 and Tranche 3 to June 30, 2028
Deferred minimum cash covenant initial testing date to July 1, 2028
items.decisionTakeaway: The amendment secures $30M of immediate liquidity and extends future draw windows and covenant testing, meaningfully lengthening Disc Medicine's debt-funded runway without an equity raise.

items.name: The Brink's Company
items.headline: Brink's shareholders overwhelmingly approve NCR Atleos acquisition
whatChanged: Brink's and NCR Atleos shareholders both voted overwhelmingly to approve the previously announced acquisition of NCR Atleos by Brink's at special meetings on June 30, 2026
Transaction has received Hart-Scott-Rodino antitrust clearance, removing a key regulatory hurdle
Deal expected to close by end of Q1 2027, subject to remaining regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions

Prior 8-K (June 18) disclosed two stockholder lawsuits and demand letters challenging the merger; companies supplemented proxy disclosures to moot claims — vote proceeded on schedule despite litigation
items.decisionTakeaway: Shareholder approval and HSR clearance remove the two biggest closing-condition risks for Brink's transformative NCR Atleos acquisition, leaving only remaining regulatory approvals before an expected Q1 2027 close.

items.name: Progress Software Corporation
items.headline: PRGS Q2 FY26: Revenue +7% YoY, License Revenue Surges 36%, 2026 Notes Redeemed
whatChanged: Q2 total revenue $253.5M (+7% YoY / +6% constant currency), driven by software licenses +36% YoY to $69.0M while maintenance/SaaS/professional services declined 1% to $184.5M
Net income rose to $21.1M ($0.50 diluted EPS) from $17.0M ($0.39) prior-year Q2; H1 net income $43.9M vs $28.0M, with operating cash flow nearly doubling to $177.5M
Redeemed $360M of 1.0% convertible senior notes due 2026 in April, funded by drawing on revolving credit facility; revolver balance increased from $600M to $850M at 5.37% rate
Effective tax rate jumped to 27% from 14% due to jurisdictional earnings mix shift and $0.8M discrete tax expense vs $1.1M discrete benefit prior year
ARR grew 2% YoY to $868M; net retention rate remained 99-100%; $147.5M remaining under share repurchase authorization after $55.1M of H1 buybacks
items.decisionTakeaway: Q2 results show strong license revenue growth offset by flat recurring revenue and a higher tax rate, while the refinancing of 2026 convertible notes onto a higher-rate revolver increases interest expense sensitivity going forward.

items.name: Constellation Brands, Inc.
items.headline: STZ Q1 FY27: Organic Net Sales +3%, Raises Reported EPS Guidance
whatChanged: Q1 FY27 reported EPS $3.79 (+31% YoY) and comparable EPS $3.43 (+7% YoY); organic net sales grew 3% despite reported net sales declining 3% due to 2025 Wine Divestitures

Raised FY27 reported EPS guidance to $11.50–$12.20 (from $11.10–$11.80 in prior 8-K), driven by a lower reported tax rate outlook of ~17% (vs ~20% previously); comparable EPS outlook affirmed at $11.20–$11.90

Beer Business net sales +2% YoY to $2.28B with operating income +2%; depletions declined 0.3% as Modelo Especial (~-2%) and Corona Extra (>-5%) declines were partially offset by Pacifico (+21%) and Victoria (+14%)
Wine and Spirits organic net sales +8% and depletions +6.6%; reported net sales fell 47% reflecting the 2025 Wine Divestitures; segment operating loss narrowed to $(1.1)M from $(6.0)M
Nicholas Fink named President & CEO (replacing Bill Newlands from prior filing); returned over $400M to shareholders via $324M buybacks YTD and dividends
items.decisionTakeaway: STZ delivered organic growth and raised reported EPS guidance, but the guidance increase is tax-driven with comparable EPS affirmed, while beer depletions turned slightly negative and reported revenue still declined 3%.

items.name: Xeris Biopharma Holdings, Inc.
items.headline: Xeris adds director Kreher, names CEO Shannon as Board Chair
whatChanged: Board expanded from 7 to 8 members; Dr. Nerissa Kreher appointed as independent Class III director effective July 1, 2026
CEO John Shannon appointed Chairperson of the Board; Marla Persky named Lead Independent Director
Kreher granted initial equity award (options + RSUs) with aggregate grant-date fair value of ~$450,000
Annual equity grants going forward valued at ~$300,000 per year under non-employee director compensation program
No committees assigned to Kreher at this time
items.decisionTakeaway: The board expansion adds clinical-development expertise (Kreher's deep biotech CMO background) while consolidating leadership under CEO Shannon as Chair, a governance shift that is unlikely to move the stock but signals continued board strengthening.

items.name: Lattice Semiconductor Corporation
items.headline: Lattice secures $950M delayed draw term loan to fund AMI acquisition
whatChanged: Entered Second Amended and Restated Credit Agreement with $200M revolver and $950M delayed draw term loan facility, maturing June 30, 2031
Delayed draw term loan proceeds earmarked to fund cash consideration and refinance AMI's outstanding debt for the previously announced AMI acquisition
No loans drawn at closing; ticking fee of 0.25% per annum on undrawn term loan commitment until funding or termination
Delayed draw commitment terminates earliest of Nov 9, 2026, AMI deal termination, or consummation without use of term loan
Interest at term SOFR + 1.00%-1.75% margin based on leverage ratio; quarterly amortization starting at 1.25% of original principal
items.decisionTakeaway: Lattice has locked in committed debt financing of up to $950M for the AMI acquisition, removing near-term funding uncertainty ahead of the Nov 9, 2026 outside date for drawing the term loan.

keyMetrics.ticking_fee: 0.25% per annum on undrawn term loan

items.name: Camden National Corporation
items.headline: Camden National declares $0.42 quarterly dividend
whatChanged: Declared quarterly dividend of $0.42 per share, payable July 31, 2026 to shareholders of record July 15, 2026
Dividend rate unchanged from the $1.68 annualized rate disclosed in the June 2026 investor presentation
items.decisionTakeaway: Camden National maintained its quarterly dividend at $0.42 per share, signaling no change in capital return policy.

items.name: Ecovyst Inc.
items.headline: Ecovyst adds $100M incremental term loan to fund INEOS Calabrian acquisition
whatChanged: Fourth Amendment to existing Term Loan Credit Agreement adds $100M in 2026 Incremental Term Loans, fully committed by Citibank, N.A.
Proceeds will finance Ecovyst's acquisition of INEOS Calabrian Holdings Corp. and INEOS Calabrian Corporation Canada, Inc. pursuant to a Share Purchase Agreement dated May 1, 2026
Incremental term loans will be fungible with and added to the existing Initial Term Loan tranche on identical terms
Condition to effectiveness includes consummation of the INEOS Calabrian acquisition substantially simultaneously with the initial borrowing
items.decisionTakeaway: Ecovyst secured $100M of incremental term loan financing to fund its previously announced INEOS Calabrian acquisition, increasing total debt but enabling an inorganic growth transaction.

keyMetrics.acquisitionTarget: INEOS Calabrian Holdings Corp. and INEOS Calabrian Corporation Canada, Inc.

items.name: Terex Corporation
items.headline: Terex stockholders approve 2026 Omnibus Incentive Plan
whatChanged: Terex established the 2026 Omnibus Incentive Plan, effective upon stockholder approval, replacing the prior 2018 Terex and 2016 Rev Group omnibus plans
Plan authorizes 3,200,000 shares for issuance (plus rollover of unused/forfeited shares from prior plans), with a 10-year term
Annual per-participant award limits set at 750,000 shares for options/SARs, 750,000 for restricted stock/RSUs, and $20M for performance or cash-based awards
Minimum one-year vesting required for all awards, with up to 5% exempt; change-in-control triggers immediate vesting at target
items.decisionTakeaway: Stockholder approval of the 2026 Omnibus Incentive Plan refreshes Terex's equity compensation capacity with 3.2M new authorized shares and standard terms, a routine governance item with no immediate earnings or cash-flow impact.

items.name: Frontier Group Holdings, Inc.
items.headline: Frontier extends Barclays credit card affinity deal to 2037, expands miles facility to $375M
whatChanged: Seventh Amendment to Credit Card Affinity Agreement with Barclays extends term from Dec 31, 2029 to June 30, 2037
Pre-purchased miles facility maximum increased from $200M to $375M, with term also extended to June 30, 2037
Enhanced net compensation expected for Frontier; pre-paid consideration received before end of June 2026, slightly better than anticipated
Facility payback begins June 2036 in 12 equal monthly installments; certain financial covenants amended
items.decisionTakeaway: Frontier materially extended and deepened its Barclays co-branded credit card partnership through 2037 with a larger $375M miles facility and improved economics, providing long-term loyalty-program revenue visibility and incremental near-term liquidity.

items.name: Progress Software Corporation
items.headline: Q2 revenue beats internal guidance; FY26 outlook raised on AI demand
whatChanged: Q2 revenue of $253.5M grew 7% YoY, ahead of company guidance of $240–$246M, driven by AI-powered product offerings

FY2026 guidance raised: revenue to $990–$1,002M (from $988–$1,000M) and non-GAAP EPS to $6.09–$6.21 (from $5.91–$6.03); GAAP EPS guidance lowered to $1.60–$1.74 (from $1.71–$1.87) due to higher tax rate of 25% (vs 23%)
Repurchased $35M of shares during the quarter and paid down $360M of convertible senior notes using revolver, bringing net leverage to 2.9x
ARR of $868M grew 2% YoY (constant currency); net retention rate was 100%
Adjusted free cash flow of $79.2M, up 114% YoY; FY2026 AFCF guidance raised to $271–$283M (from $263–$275M)
items.decisionTakeaway: Progress beat its own Q2 revenue guidance on AI-driven demand and raised full-year non-GAAP EPS and cash flow guidance, though GAAP EPS guidance was cut by a higher tax rate and ARR growth remains modest at 2%.

items.name: Eagle Bancorp, Inc.
items.headline: EagleBank settles DOJ AML probe with non-prosecution agreement
whatChanged: EagleBank reached a settlement with the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Middle District of Pennsylvania regarding its AML controls investigation and relationship with a former customer convicted of bank fraud in 2020
Company and Bank entered a one-year non-prosecution agreement with DOJ, under which no criminal or civil charges will be brought subject to compliance with agreement terms
Bank will pay approximately $9.8 million, which was fully accrued in the company's audited FY2025 financial statements
No new financial charge is required as the settlement amount was already reserved in prior-year audited results
items.decisionTakeaway: The DOJ AML investigation has resolved via a non-prosecution agreement with a $9.8M payment already accrued in FY2025, eliminating a legal overhang without any incremental earnings impact.

keyMetrics.settlementPayment: ~$9.8 million (fully accrued in FY2025 audited financial statements)

items.name: MarineMax, Inc.
items.headline: MarineMax refinances $1.49B credit facilities, extends maturities to 2031
whatChanged: Refinanced $1.49B senior secured credit facilities with new maturity of June 2031, extending debt maturity profile by five years
Revolver expanded from $100M to $150M, increasing financial flexibility
Mortgage facility reduced from $100M to $85M delayed draw, with $35M currently outstanding
Company states the refinancing reduces borrowing costs and improves terms
Facilities led by M&T Bank as Administrative Agent and Wells Fargo as Joint Lead Arranger
items.decisionTakeaway: MarineMax locked in a five-year maturity extension on $1.49B of debt through June 2031 while lowering borrowing costs and expanding its revolver by $50M, materially de-risking its near-term refinancing wall and improving liquidity headroom.

items.name: Apogee Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Apogee Q1 FY27 swings to profit; announces $105M KCI acquisition
whatChanged: Net earnings swung to $11.5M ($0.54 diluted EPS) from a $2.7M loss ($0.13) prior year, as prior-year Project Fortify Phase 2 restructuring charges ($15.3M) did not recur; operating income rose to $18.8M from $6.9M

Adjusted EBITDA fell 6.6% to $32.1M (margin 9.4% vs 9.9%) as Architectural Glass EBITDA dropped 56% on lower volume and material inflation, partially offset by Architectural Metals EBITDA up 46% on productivity savings
Definitive agreement to acquire Keller Companies, Inc. for ~$105M cash plus up to $10M earn-out, expected to close early July; to be integrated into Architectural Glass Segment
Architectural Services backlog grew to $734.5M from $682.9M YoY; segment sales rose 8.2%
Repurchased 269,500 shares for $9.7M (none in prior-year quarter); dividend raised to $0.27/share from $0.26
items.decisionTakeaway: The return to GAAP profitability is driven mainly by absence of prior-year restructuring charges while adjusted EBITDA declined, and the pending $105M KCI acquisition signals continued portfolio expansion into Architectural Glass despite that segment's sharp margin compression.

items.name: First Financial Corporation
items.headline: New employment agreement for SVP and Chief Lending Officer Franklin
whatChanged: First Financial entered a new employment agreement effective July 1, 2026 with Mark A. Franklin as SVP and Chief Lending Officer of both the Bank and the Corporation, running through June 30, 2028

Franklin's base salary set at $319,307 per annum (effective Jan 1, 2026), with target annual bonus of at least 35% of base salary and target long-term incentive equity grant of at least 40% of base salary
Change-in-control severance multiplier is 2.0x vs 2.99x for the CEO agreement, reflecting the lower executive tier
Noncompete restricted area is Bloomington, Indiana and a 75-mile radius (50 miles if terminated without cause), vs Terre Haute for the CEO
Franklin's agreement does not include the CEO-specific benefits: life insurance premium payments, post-retirement health coverage, country club membership, or board nomination rights
items.decisionTakeaway: The filing formalizes retention terms for an existing senior lending officer with modest compensation relative to the CEO, signaling continuity in the lending leadership rather than any strategic or financial shift.

items.name: First Financial Corporation
items.headline: THFF enters new CEO employment agreement through June 2028
whatChanged: New employment agreement dated June 29, 2026 for Norman D. Lowery as President and CEO of both First Financial Bank, N.A. and First Financial Corporation, with term through June 30, 2028
Base salary set at $698,987 per annum with target annual bonus of at least 60% of base salary and target long-term incentive equity grant of at least 80% of base salary
Change-in-control severance provides lump sum equal to 2.99x the sum of base salary, target bonus, annual benefit amount, COBRA premiums, and top-up payment, subject to OCC limits
Post-termination non-compete restricted to one year within 75-mile radius of Terre Haute, IN (50 miles if terminated without cause)
Agreement includes continued life insurance premium payments, post-retirement Medicare coverage for employee and spouse, company vehicle, and country club membership benefits
items.decisionTakeaway: The filing establishes a renewed two-year employment contract for the incumbent CEO with defined compensation and change-in-control severance terms, providing management continuity but no material change to the company's outlook or capital structure.

keyMetrics.changeInControlSeveranceMultiple: 2.99x (base salary + target bonus + annual benefit amount + 12x COBRA + top-up)

items.name: Conduent Incorporated
items.headline: Conduent to sell Tolling Business to Quarterhill for $70M cash plus 7% equity
whatChanged: Conduent signed an Asset Purchase Agreement to sell its Tolling Business to Quarterhill Inc. for $70M cash plus common shares equal to 7% of Quarterhill's outstanding shares pre-closing
Transaction expected to close in Q4 2026, subject to customary closing conditions including competition/foreign investment approvals and TSX listing approval for the stock consideration
This follows the May 21, 2026 agreement to sell the Transit Business for $164M, continuing Conduent's divestiture of its transportation-related segments
CBS agreed to a customary non-compete preventing Conduent from participating in certain competitive activities in the tolling space
items.decisionTakeaway: Conduent is monetizing its Tolling Business for $70M cash plus a 7% equity stake in Quarterhill, marking a second transportation-segment divestiture in five weeks that collectively yields ~$234M in cash and stock against a $226M market cap, signaling a significant portfolio reshaping.

keyMetrics.purchasePrice_stock: 7% of Buyer's issued and outstanding common shares (pre-closing)

items.name: OceanFirst Financial Corp.
items.headline: 8-K/A corrects rent-regulated loan sale figure from $736M to $836M
whatChanged: Amendment No. 1 to the June 29, 2026 8-K filed solely to correct the stated amount of loan collateral with rent-regulated exposure sold
Rent-regulated exposure in the sold $1.3B NYC multifamily portfolio revised upward from $736M to $836M, a $100M increase
No other changes to the Original Form 8-K, which announced completion of the previously announced $1.3B loan sale
items.decisionTakeaway: The correction increases the disclosed rent-regulated portion of the sold portfolio by $100M, meaningfully raising the share of the $1.3B sale tied to rent-regulated collateral from ~57% to ~64%, but does not alter the sale price or overall transaction terms.

keyMetrics.loanCollateralWithRentRegulatedExposureS: $836 million (corrected from $736 million in original filing)

items.name: Cal-Maine Foods, Inc.
items.headline: Cal-Maine settles DOJ antitrust probe for $1.5M, no wrongdoing admitted
whatChanged: Cal-Maine reached agreement with DOJ and 17 state AGs to resolve alleged antitrust claims following a 15-month investigation into anticompetitive conduct among egg producers
Company admits no wrongdoing and was assessed no fines or penalties
Settlement terms include $1.5M payment to the states, donation of 30 million eggs, and implementation of antitrust compliance and reporting measures
Resolution removes a significant legal/regulatory overhang at negligible financial cost relative to company scale
Agreement remains subject to applicable court approvals and procedures
items.decisionTakeaway: Cal-Maine resolved a 15-month DOJ antitrust investigation with no admission of wrongdoing and only $1.5M plus egg donations, eliminating a key legal overhang at immaterial cost.

items.name: Autoliv, Inc.
items.headline: Autoliv Americas President Kevin Fox to Resign
whatChanged: Kevin Fox resigning as President, Autoliv Americas for personal reasons; remains through August 31, 2026
Fox to serve as executive senior advisor to CEO through February 28, 2027 to support transition
Anthony Nellis (EVP Legal Affairs, General Counsel, Secretary) named acting President, Autoliv Americas after August 31, 2026
Successor recruitment process launched immediately
items.decisionTakeaway: The orderly retirement of a division president with a named interim leader and transition advisory period signals managed continuity rather than a disruptive leadership void at Autoliv Americas.

items.name: Allegiant Travel Company
items.headline: Allegiant raises Q2 2026 adjusted EPS guidance to at least $1.25 post Sun Country acquisition
whatChanged: Company now expects Q2 2026 combined-entity adjusted EPS of at least $1.25, including Sun Country results from May 13–June 30, 2026
Updated outlook sharply exceeds prior standalone Allegiant Air guidance issued April 30, 2026, which contemplated an adjusted loss per share of ~$0.50 at the midpoint
Standalone Allegiant Air Q2 TRASM now expected to increase more than 23% YoY, exceeding prior expectations
Improved outlook driven by favorable demand environment at both airlines and lower June fuel expense
Sun Country acquisition closed May 13, 2026; Q2 results will reflect consolidated performance from close through quarter end
items.decisionTakeaway: Allegiant's Q2 2026 guidance swung from a ~$0.50 standalone adjusted loss to at least $1.25 combined adjusted EPS, driven by stronger-than-expected demand and lower fuel costs, marking a materially positive earnings revision post Sun Country close.

items.name: PPL Corporation
items.headline: PPL elects Kenneth Hartwick to Board effective July 1
whatChanged: Board elected Kenneth M. Hartwick as a director, effective July 1, 2026
Hartwick is former president and CEO of Ontario Power Generation (2019–2025) with prior CFO roles at Hydro One and Just Energy
Hartwick assigned to People and Compensation Committee and Finance Committee; determined independent under NYSE rules
Press release announcing the election furnished as Exhibit 99.1 under Item 7.01
items.decisionTakeaway: PPL adds a director with deep utility-sector CEO and CFO experience, but the appointment is routine and does not alter the company's financial outlook or strategy.

items.name: Kennametal Inc.
items.headline: Kennametal names Amanda Cole as VP & CHRO
whatChanged: Amanda Cole appointed VP and Chief Human Resources Officer effective July 21, 2026, reporting to CEO Sanjay Chowbey
Cole joins from Wesco International where she was VP of HR for Electrical & Electronic Solutions and IT/Digital Functions since 2022
Judith Bacchus, current VP & Chief Administrative Officer (and CHRO since June 2011), will retire on or about October 1, 2026
Cole's compensation: $375K base salary, $58K sign-on bonus, $300K special RSU grant, and FY27 target bonus of 50% of base salary
items.decisionTakeaway: Kennametal announced a planned HR leadership transition with Amanda Cole replacing the retiring Judith Bacchus, a routine non-CEO officer change with no impact on operations or financial outlook.

items.name: Easterly Government Properties, Inc.
items.headline: DEA closes $200M five-year unsecured term loan
whatChanged: Closed new $200M senior unsecured term loan maturing June 2031, with a $50M accordion option (total potential size $250M)
Proceeds to repay outstanding borrowings on the $400M revolving credit facility and for general corporate purposes
Pricing at SOFR + 1.20%–1.70% based on leverage ratio; initial spread set at SOFR + 1.30%
CFO Marino stated the transaction enhances liquidity profile and supports future growth initiatives
items.decisionTakeaway: DEA term-out of $200M from revolver into a 5-year unsecured loan at SOFR+130bps extends its maturity profile and frees revolver capacity, modestly improving liquidity without changing leverage.

keyMetrics.spreadRange: SOFR + 1.20% to 1.70% depending on leverage ratio
keyMetrics.useOfProceeds: Repay $400M revolver borrowings and general corporate purposes

items.name: Sharplink, Inc.
items.headline: Sharplink Buys 10,000 ETH, Repurchases 2.1M Shares After $75M Raise
whatChanged: Purchased 10,000 ETH at an average price of ~$1,611/ETH, bringing total ETH holdings to 886,725 as of June 28, 2026 (up from 875,776 reported June 16)
Repurchased 2,132,773 shares at an average price of $4.69/share, bringing cumulative buybacks to 4,071,223 shares since program inception in August 2025
Closed the $75 million registered direct offering of common stock and warrants announced in the prior 8-K
CEO Joseph Chalom reiterated capital allocation objective to increase ETH per share, stating shares are significantly undervalued
items.decisionTakeaway: Sharplink has deployed proceeds from its $75M premium-priced equity raise into both additional ETH treasury accumulation and open-market share repurchases, executing on its stated accretive ETH-per-share strategy.

keyMetrics.total_ETH_holdings: 886,725 ETH as of June 28, 2026 (vs 875,776 as of June 16, 2026)

items.name: LCI Industries
items.headline: LCII to merge with Patrick Industries in all-stock deal
whatChanged: Definitive agreement: LCI shareholders receive 1.2440 Patrick (PATK) shares per LCII share; Patrick holders ~52%, LCI holders ~48% of combined company
$150M+ estimated run-rate cost synergies expected within three years of closing, from procurement, SG&A, engineering, and supply chain
Pro forma trailing-twelve-months (as of March 2026): ~$8.1B revenue, ~$1.0B adjusted EBITDA (incl. synergies), ~$508M free cash flow (incl. synergies), pro forma net leverage 2.1x
Leadership: Patrick CEO Andy Nemeth to lead combined company; Todd Cleveland (Patrick) as Chair, Johnny Sirpilla (LCI interim CEO) as Vice Chair; 12-member board split 6-6
Expected close in 1H 2027, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals
items.decisionTakeaway: LCII is combining with Patrick Industries in an all-stock merger at 1.2440x exchange ratio with $150M+ in targeted synergies and a pro forma $8.1B revenue platform, fundamentally reshaping the company's scale and diversification.

items.name: Akebia Therapeutics, Inc.
items.headline: VOICE Trial Stopped Early: Vafseo Shows Safety Superiority Over ESA
whatChanged: VOICE trial (2,116 patients) stopped early after interim analysis met prespecified stopping criteria, with Vafseo demonstrating superiority over ESA on composite endpoint of all-cause mortality and hospitalization
Win odds of 1.16 (95% CI 1.06–1.28, p=0.0016) established both non-inferiority and superiority for the primary composite endpoint

Hospitalizations significantly lower with Vafseo vs ESA (1.11 vs 1.23 per patient-year, IRR 0.90, 95% CI 0.824–0.988); mortality showed no significant difference (8.77% vs 8.78% per 100 patient-years)
Results replicate safety outcomes from the INNO 2 VATE program; Akebia and USRC plan to submit data to an upcoming scientific meeting
items.decisionTakeaway: Positive interim VOICE data showing Vafseo reduces hospitalizations versus ESAs strengthens the clinical differentiation case for Akebia's sole commercial product and could support accelerated U.S. uptake among dialysis providers.

items.name: QXO, Inc.
items.headline: QXO tender offer for TopBuild notes expires with 99.5%+ participation
whatChanged: Tender offers for TopBuild's $500M 4.125% Senior Notes due 2032 and $750M 5.625% Senior Notes due 2034 expired June 29, 2026; $497.7M (99.54%) of 2032 Notes and $748.1M (99.75%) of 2034 Notes were validly tendered and accepted for purchase

Consent solicitations succeeded: supplemental indentures executed to eliminate change-of-control offer requirements, restrictive covenants, and most events of default, becoming operative on the Settlement Date
Settlement Date expected July 1, 2026, substantially coinciding with and contingent upon closing of the TopBuild Acquisition
Conditional redemption notices issued June 18, 2026 for any remaining outstanding Notes at $1,011.25 per $1,000 principal, also conditioned on tender offer consummation
Early tender price was $1,011.25 per $1,000 principal; late tender price was $961.25 per $1,000 principal
items.decisionTakeaway: Near-total tender participation (99.5%+) and successful consent solicitations remove debt-related obstacles to the TopBuild acquisition, which is expected to close on or about July 1, 2026.

items.name: Stoke Therapeutics, Inc.
items.headline: Stoke Completes Phase 3 EMPEROR Enrollment, Targets Q1 2027 NDA Filing
whatChanged: Completed enrollment of 162 patients into the pivotal Phase 3 EMPEROR study of zorevunersen for Dravet syndrome in 10 months
Plans to initiate a rolling U.S. NDA submission to the FDA in Q1 2027, with Phase 3 data readout anticipated in Q3 2027 to complete the rolling NDA submission in H2 2027
Approximately 50 of 162 patients are through 28 weeks of treatment (primary endpoint measurement point); no patients have discontinued treatment
Additional cohort of ~30 patients enrolling in Europe with last patient expected to enroll in August; data from European and Chinese patients not planned for inclusion in U.S. NDA submission
Zorevunersen recently granted Breakthrough Therapy Status in China; potential FDA approval and U.S. launch targeted by early 2028
items.decisionTakeaway: Completion of Phase 3 enrollment with zero discontinuations and a defined regulatory timeline (rolling NDA Q1 2027, data readout Q3 2027, potential launch early 2028) meaningfully de-risks the zorevunersen development pathway and clarifies the near-term catalyst calendar.

keyMetrics.Additional European cohort: ~30 patients (last patient expected to enroll August 2026)

items.name: Joby Aviation, Inc.
items.headline: Joby-Toyota manufacturing JV stockholders agreement filed
whatChanged: Joby and Toyota formed a Delaware joint venture, 'Joby Toyota Aero Manufacturing Preparation Company,' to manufacture the S4 Series eVTOL aircraft
Toyota committed to purchase 1,020,000 shares of JV common stock for $1,020,000; Joby committed to purchase 980,000 shares for $980,000, at $1.00 per share
Board composition: 3 Toyota-designated directors and 2 Joby-designated directors, with mutual consent required for major actions including equity issuance, business plan changes, and IPO
Mandatory capital contributions tied to funding milestones in Schedule 4, with default provisions allowing the non-defaulting party to fund the other's portion
IP framework distinguishes background IP, licensed Toyota manufacturing IP, and jointly developed aircraft/manufacturing IP among the parties
items.decisionTakeaway: Joby has formalized a manufacturing JV with Toyota to produce the S4 Series eVTOL, deepening the Toyota partnership and establishing a funded production vehicle with milestone-based capital contributions.

items.name: Palvella Therapeutics, Inc.
items.headline: Palvella appoints Matthew Pauls as new director, expands board to eight
whatChanged: Board expanded from seven to eight members; Matthew Pauls appointed as Class I director with term expiring at 2027 Annual Meeting
Pauls is current CEO of Savara (SVRA) and former CEO of Strongbridge Biopharma, bringing rare-disease biopharma commercialization and M&A experience
Pauls granted option to purchase 6,000 shares of common stock, vesting in equal monthly installments over 36 months subject to continued service
Board determined Pauls is independent under Nasdaq listing rules; no related party transactions disclosed
items.decisionTakeaway: Palvella added an experienced rare-disease biopharma CEO/director to its board, modestly strengthening commercialization and deal expertise ahead of potential pipeline milestones.

items.name: Jabil Inc.
items.headline: Jabil Q3 FY26 revenue up 12% YoY, AI infrastructure drives growth
whatChanged: Net revenue rose to $8.75B in Q3 FY26 from $7.83B YoY (+11.8%), with nine-month revenue at $25.3B vs $21.6B (+17.6%); diluted EPS was $2.59 vs $2.03 YoY for the quarter and $6.01 vs $3.94 for nine months

Intelligent Infrastructure segment revenue surged to $4.17B in Q3 (vs $3.43B YoY) and $12.05B for nine months (vs $8.58B YoY), with segment income rising to $256M from $181M in Q3 — the clear growth engine

Completed Hanley Energy Group acquisition for $752M cash on Jan 2, 2026 (allocated to Intelligent Infrastructure, $340M goodwill) and Rebound Technologies for $133M on Sep 1, 2025; goodwill increased from $841M to $1.228B

Issued $500M 4.200% notes due 2029 and $500M 4.750% notes due 2033 in Jan 2026, using proceeds to repay $500M 1.700% notes due April 2026; 2026 share repurchase program has $891M utilized of $1B authorized with $109M remaining

Sales cycle improved dramatically to 5 days in Q3 FY26 from 24 days YoY, reflecting working capital efficiency gains; 2025 Restructuring Plan (~$200M) substantially complete, with Q3 restructuring charges falling to $7M from $16M YoY
items.decisionTakeaway: Jabil's Q3 FY26 results show accelerating revenue growth led by Intelligent Infrastructure (AI/data center), supported by the Hanley acquisition, while margin expansion and sharply lower restructuring charges signal improving operational leverage.

items.name: Pacira BioSciences, Inc.
items.headline: Pacira divests iovera° cryoanalgesia business to Zimmer Biomet for up to $140M
whatChanged: Pacira agreed to sell its iovera° handheld cryoanalgesia device business and Pacira CryoTech subsidiary to Zimmer Biomet for up to $140M total
Upfront payment of $70M plus up to $70M in contingent revenue-based milestones through 2031 ($18.5M at ≥$50M revenue, $23.5M at ≥$60M, $28M at ≥$70M in any annual period 2027–2031)
66 employees (~8% of workforce) expected to transfer to Zimmer upon closing
Transaction expected to close in Q3 2026, subject to customary closing conditions
Post-closing restrictive covenants include non-competition, non-solicitation, and confidentiality obligations
items.decisionTakeaway: Pacira is monetizing its non-core iovera° device franchise for $70M upfront (≈7% of market cap) plus up to $70M in milestones, sharpening focus on its core Exparel business while adding meaningful cash.

keyMetrics.milestone1: $18.5M if net revenue ≥$50M in any applicable period 2027–2031

items.name: Ingredion Incorporated
items.headline: Ingredion completes $165M sale of 51% Rafhan Maize stake
whatChanged: Ingredion completed the sale of 51% of Rafhan Maize Products Co. Ltd. (Pakistan) to Nishat Hotels and Properties Limited / Nishat Group affiliates for ~$165M cash
Post-transaction Ingredion retains ~20% minority interest in Rafhan Maize with a put option exercisable beginning in year 5 post-closing
Rafhan Maize generated ~$250M net sales in 2025 and is not part of a reportable segment

Ancillary commercial agreements include a manufacturing/supply agreement (Rafhan supplying Ingredion affiliates) and a distribution agreement (Ingredion supplying specialty products to Rafhan for Pakistan distribution)
items.decisionTakeaway: Ingredion monetized a non-core Pakistan asset for ~$165M cash while retaining a 20% minority stake with a put option and ongoing supply/distribution agreements, modestly streamlining the portfolio and adding liquidity.

items.name: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Eos forms JV with Cerberus and Hudson Bay, targets $150M rights offering
whatChanged: Entered binding amended and restated term sheet with CCM Frontier (Cerberus) and HBC (Hudson Bay) to form Frontier Power USA Parent JV; CCM Frontier contributes $100M for Class A-2 Units plus founder's equity, HBC contributes $50M for Class C Units
Eos to fund its JV investment via a $150M target rights offering at $5.481/unit (1 share + 0.4388 warrant per unit); warrants exercisable at $5.481, 10-year expiry, cashless basis
Company to issue CCM Warrant for 20,017,772 shares and HBC Warrant for 10,008,886 shares, both at $5.481 exercise price, 10-year expiry
HBC holds exchange right on Class C Units into EOSE shares at $15/$17.50/$20 thresholds pre-Dec 31 2026, then at Rights Offering final pricing thereafter
Obtained Second and Third DOE Limited Consents and CCM Lender consent for the Frontier Transactions; CFO changed from Nathan Kroeker (Interim) to Alessandro Lagi
items.decisionTakeaway: Eos secured DOE and Cerberus lender consent to form a JV capitalized with $250M from Cerberus/Hudson Bay plus a contemplated $150M rights offering, but the structure entails substantial warrant dilution (~30M shares at $5.481) and cedes JV board control to Cerberus (4 of 7 seats).

keyMetrics.CCM founder equity: 50,000,001 Class A-1 Units for Pre-Closing Contribution

items.name: Aflac Incorporated
items.headline: Aflac Japan discloses cybersecurity breach involving customer data
whatChanged: Aflac Japan discovered unauthorized third-party access to certain systems between June 15-25, 2026, and suspended affected systems upon identification
Impacted files contain policy and coverage details, personal information, and bank account information
Aflac Japan has notified the Japan Financial Services Agency and other relevant authorities; individual notifications are planned
Incident is limited to Japan systems; U.S. business systems were not accessed
Full scope and potential ultimate impact on the Company are not yet known; investigation remains ongoing with third-party cybersecurity experts engaged
items.decisionTakeaway: A data breach at Aflac Japan exposing policy, personal, and bank account information creates unresolved regulatory, litigation, and reputational risk for the company's largest market segment, with financial impact still undetermined.

items.name: Realty Income Corporation
items.headline: Realty Income prices $600M 3.625% notes due 2032
whatChanged: Realty Income entered a purchase agreement dated June 29, 2026 to issue $600 million of 3.625% senior unsecured notes due 2032
Underwriting syndicate led by Barclays, BNP Paribas, RBC Europe, Banco Santander, and Wells Fargo Securities International
Closing scheduled for July 7, 2026; notes issued under existing S-3 shelf registration and 1998 indenture
Coupon of 3.625% is notably below the 4.750% rate on the $800M 2033 notes issued in April 2026 per the prior 8-K
Notes rank pari passu with existing unsecured indebtedness and senior to any subordinated debt
items.decisionTakeaway: Realty Income is raising $600M of 7-year unsecured debt at 3.625%, a lower coupon than its April 2026 issuance, signaling continued capital-markets access at improving borrowing costs for the REIT's funding needs.

items.name: News Corporation

Approximately US$664M remains available under the US$1B authorization
items.decisionTakeaway: News Corp continues steady daily repurchases under its $1B buyback program, now ~34% utilized with no change in pace or terms.

items.name: News Corporation

items.name: Martin Marietta Materials, Inc.
items.headline: MLM files definitive $13.5B Lhoist North America acquisition agreement
whatChanged: Martin Marietta entered a definitive Securities Sale Agreement to acquire 100% of Lhoist North America, Inc. from LNA Holding SRL for ~$13.5B in cash and stock, with a Locked Box Date of Dec 31, 2025 and Long Stop Date of Oct 31, 2026 (extendable to Mar 31, 2027)

Cash consideration base amount is $6,109,241,272 plus a daily Additional Amount of $1,213,051 per day from the Locked Box Date through Completion; stock consideration is 10,953,543 newly issued MLM common shares valued at ~$6.5B
A $350M Regulatory Termination Fee is payable by MLM to the Vendor if the deal fails to close due to regulatory conditions by the Extended Long Stop Date
LNA generated $1.8B in gross sales and $786M Adjusted EBITDA for TTM ended Dec 31, 2025; deal implies ~15x Adjusted EBITDA including ~$85M run-rate cost synergies
Combined Net Leverage expected at ~3.7x at closing with a target to reduce below 2.5x within 24 months; Berghmans family to own ~15% of MLM post-close with one board seat and one observer
items.decisionTakeaway: The definitive agreement locks in a transformative $13.5B cash-and-stock acquisition that makes MLM the nation's leading lime producer but pushes leverage to ~3.7x, with a $350M regulatory break fee and HSR approval as the key closing conditions.

keyMetrics.cashConsideration: $7.0 billion (Base Amount $6,109,241,272 + $1,213,051/day from Locked Box Date to Completion)
keyMetrics.berghmansFamilyOwnership: ~15% of MLM on fully diluted basis post-closing

items.name: Globe Life Inc.
items.headline: Globe Life enters $1B amended credit agreement
whatChanged: Globe Life Inc. entered into a Third Amended and Restated Credit Agreement dated June 26, 2026, with $1.0 billion in aggregate commitments, replacing the prior Second Amended and Restated Credit Agreement dated March 29, 2024.
Wells Fargo Bank replaces Bank of America as Administrative Agent, Swing Line Lender, and L/C Administrator under the new agreement.

The facility includes a pricing grid tied to the Borrower's S&P/Moody's debt ratings, with facility fees ranging from 0.07% to 0.15% and Term SOFR loan spreads ranging from 0.805% to 1.35% depending on rating level.
TMK Re, Ltd., Globe Life's Bermuda reinsurance subsidiary, joins as a Loan Party alongside the Borrower.
The agreement is an amendment and restatement, not a novation, of the existing credit facility.
items.decisionTakeaway: Globe Life refinanced its $1.0B revolving credit facility with a new Third Amended and Restated Credit Agreement, switching administrative agents to Wells Fargo and maintaining the same commitment size, which preserves liquidity without materially changing the company's capital structure.

items.name: Digital Realty Trust, Inc.
items.headline: DLR Buys Blackstone Stake in 3 Virginia Data Centers for $3.5B
whatChanged: DLR agreed to purchase Blackstone's blended 64% equity interest in three fully leased Northern Virginia data centers (288 MW total IT capacity) at a gross value of $7.8 billion
Total consideration to Blackstone is $3.5 billion, comprising $1.2 billion cash and $2.3 billion in DLR shares
Portfolio is 100% leased to three investment-grade hyperscale customers under 15-year leases with 3.6% annual rent escalators and blended AA- credit rating
Expected initial stabilized cap rate of over 6.5%; two centers stabilize H1 2027, third in H1 2028
Transaction expected to be accretive to Core FFO per share in both 2027 and 2028
items.decisionTakeaway: DLR is consolidating ownership of 288 MW of fully-leased hyperscale capacity in Northern Virginia for $3.5B paid in cash and stock, adding long-duration AA-leased revenue that is accretive to Core FFO in 2027–2028.

keyMetrics.leaseTerms: 15-year leases, 3.6% annual rent escalators, blended AA- credit rating
keyMetrics.accretion: Accretive to Core FFO per share in 2027 and 2028

items.name: Corteva, Inc.
items.headline: Corteva names future crop protection board; separation on track for 4Q 2026
whatChanged: Corteva announced a nine-person board for the standalone crop protection company post-separation, with Greg Page as Independent Chair
Luke Kissam appointed as future CEO of the crop protection company, effective at separation
Existing board members Karen Grimes, Marcos Lutz, Chuck Magro, and Kerry Preete will not continue with the crop protection company
Separation of seed and crop protection businesses remains on track for 4Q 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: The full board and CEO for the post-separation crop protection company are now identified, confirming the 4Q 2026 spin remains on track and reducing execution uncertainty around leadership transitions.

items.name: SSR Mining Inc.
items.headline: SSR Mining completes $1.5B Çöpler divestiture, files pro forma financials
whatChanged: Completed sale of 80% interest in Çöpler mine (Türkiye) to Cengiz Holding for $1.5B on June 24, 2026, per March 24, 2026 share purchase agreement
Pro forma balance sheet at March 31, 2026 shows cash increasing to $2.13B from $634M as reported, with total assets declining to $5.18B from $5.95B as Çöpler assets/liabilities removed
Pro forma continuing operations EPS for FY2025: diluted $2.46 vs $1.85 as reported; FY2024: diluted $0.76 vs $(1.29) as reported; FY2023: diluted $0.37 vs $(0.48) as reported
Estimated loss on transaction of ~$2.2M, with net cash proceeds of ~$1.49B after $5.7M working capital adjustment and $5.0M transaction costs
Çöpler already classified as discontinued operations in Q1 2026; pro forma removes Çöpler results from FY2023-FY2025 income statements
items.decisionTakeaway: SSR Mining has closed the $1.5B Çöpler divestiture, nearly tripling pro forma cash to $2.13B and revealing that continuing operations are materially more profitable than consolidated figures showed, with pro forma FY2025 diluted EPS of $2.46 vs $1.85 reported.

keyMetrics.proFormaRevenue_FY2025: $1,629,637K (unchanged from reported; Çöpler already discontinued ops)
keyMetrics.transactionCosts: ~$5.0M ($1.9M incurred in Q1 2026, $3.1M estimated at closing)
keyMetrics.plerWriteDown_Q1_2026: $338.2M to fair value less costs to sell

items.name: Iridium Communications Inc.
items.headline: Rocket Lab to acquire Iridium for $54/share, ~$8B EV
whatChanged: Iridium entered into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Rocket Lab Corporation at $54 per share, half cash and half Rocket Lab stock, implying ~$8B enterprise value
Deal represents a ~24% premium to Friday's closing price and ~89% above the share price before Globalstar was acquired by Amazon
A collar feature protects Iridium shareholders from large swings in Rocket Lab stock price between signing and expected mid-2027 close
Iridium RSUs will convert to Rocket Lab RSUs at closing and continue vesting on their normal schedule
Strategic rationale: vertical integration — Rocket Lab can build, launch, and operate satellites, positioning the combined entity as an end-to-end space company
items.decisionTakeaway: Rocket Lab's $54/share cash-and-stock offer for Iridium at a ~24% premium and ~$8B EV is a transformative vertical-integration play, but the deal's ultimate value depends on Rocket Lab's share price through a collar and regulatory approval expected by mid-2027.

items.name: ON Semiconductor Corporation
items.headline: onsemi CEO details Synaptics acquisition rationale at employee all-hands
whatChanged: onsemi filed a Rule 425 transcript of a June 29, 2026 employee All Hands where CEO Hassane El-Khoury discussed the proposed acquisition of Synaptics Incorporated

Strategic rationale: adds edge AI compute (Astra platform), wireless connectivity (Wi-Fi/Bluetooth/Thread/Zigbee/GPS), and human-machine interface to onsemi's power and sensing portfolio, creating an 'intelligent systems' platform
Combined TAM expands by $30B to $243B, with a $100B subset (AI infrastructure through physical AI) growing at 25% over the next five years
Deal expected to be accretive to non-GAAP EPS within 18 months post-synergies; synergies primarily from eliminating duplicate public-company overhead, with cost synergies longer-term
Expected close mid-2027; companies must operate independently until then; S-4 proxy/prospectus to be filed
items.decisionTakeaway: onsemi's CEO framed the Synaptics deal as a strategic capability acquisition adding edge AI compute and connectivity to its power/sensing franchise, with accretion to non-GAAP EPS within 18 months and a close targeted for mid-2027.

keyMetrics.expected accretion timeline: accretive to non-GAAP EPS within 18 months post-synergies

items.name: Synaptics Incorporated
items.headline: onsemi confirms Synaptics acquisition; close expected mid-2027
whatChanged: ON Semiconductor announced a definitive agreement to acquire Synaptics, combining onsemi's intelligent power/sensing with Synaptics' edge AI compute (Astra platform), wireless connectivity, and human-machine interface capabilities
Transaction expected to close mid-2027, subject to regulatory and Synaptics stockholder approval; S-4 registration statement to be filed
Deal described as accretive to non-GAAP EPS within 18 months post-synergies, primarily from public-company overhead reduction rather than cost synergies
Combined TAM grows by $30B to $243B, with a $100B subset (AI infrastructure through physical AI) growing at 25% over five years
Cross-selling identified as a day-one capability; combined roadmap integration (e.g., analog products into onsemi manufacturing) seen as longer-term
items.decisionTakeaway: onsemi's acquisition of Synaptics is a strategic move to build an intelligent-systems platform spanning power, sensing, and edge AI compute, with EPS accretion targeted within 18 months of a mid-2027 close.

items.name: Equity Residential

Exchange ratio set at 2.793 EQR common shares per AvalonBay common share

items.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
items.headline: BlueHalo acquisition reshapes AVAV into two segments; funded backlog up 63%
whatChanged: Completed BlueHalo acquisition effective May 1, 2025, creating two reportable segments: Autonomous Systems (AxS) and Space, Cyber and Directed Energy (SCDE), significantly expanding capabilities into cyber, directed energy, and space
Funded backlog rose to $1,183.0M from $726.6M YoY; unfunded backlog rose to $1,457.7M from $774.6M, with ~85% of funded backlog expected to convert to FY2027 revenue
New risk factors highlight DOGE-related government spending scrutiny and potential Pentagon budget cuts under the Trump administration as threats to future contract funding
Disclosed material weaknesses in internal controls stemming from BlueHalo integration, requiring significant remediation resources
Revenue concentration: U.S. government ~85% of revenue (DoD ~63%), U.S. Army ~25%, foreign customers ~28% for FY2026
items.decisionTakeaway: The BlueHalo acquisition materially expanded AVAV's revenue base and backlog but introduced integration risk, internal control weaknesses, and heightened exposure to DOGE-driven government spending cuts that could pressure the large funded backlog conversion.

items.name: Minerals Technologies Inc.
items.headline: MTX files talc Chapter 11 plan, takes $290M Q2 charge
whatChanged: MTX filed a Plan of Reorganization in the BMI OldCo Chapter 11 cases to meet a Bankruptcy Court deadline; the plan includes funding a Talc Personal Injury Trust with $450M from Non-Debtor Affiliates

MTX will record a $290M charge in Q2 2026 to increase its reserve for estimated costs related to the cases

The Chapter 11 process, including plan confirmation, is recommended to be abated until the District Court makes a final determination on the central issue
items.decisionTakeaway: MTX's $290M Q2 charge and $450M trust funding commitment materially increase the talc litigation cost estimate, though the ultimate outcome hinges on the District Court's pending asbestos threshold determination.

keyMetrics.claimsWaived: >$100 million in claims against the Debtors
keyMetrics.Q2_2026_charge: $290 million to increase reserve for estimated costs

items.name: American Eagle Outfitters, Inc.

items.name: The Charles Schwab Corporation

items.name: Liberty Latin America Ltd.
items.headline: LILAK annual meeting: directors re-elected, KPMG ratified, 2026 incentive plan approved
whatChanged: Four Class III directors (Fries, de Angoitia Noriega, Gould, Jacobson) re-elected to the Board until the 2028 AGM

Liberty Latin America 2026 Incentive Plan approved, though with notable opposition: 33.3M for vs 17.5M against

items.name: Praxis Precision Medicines, Inc.
items.headline: FDA extends relutrigine PDUFA date by three months to Dec 27, 2026
whatChanged: FDA extended the review period for the relutrigine NDA by three months, moving the PDUFA target action date from September 27, 2026 to December 27, 2026.
The extension follows Praxis's submission of additional sensitivity analyses of existing clinical data, which the FDA classified as a 'major amendment.'
The FDA did not request new clinical studies and cited no safety or manufacturing concerns; the review remains active and ongoing.
The Company expressed continued confidence in the relutrigine application and is proceeding with commercial preparation activities.
items.decisionTakeaway: A three-month PDUFA delay for relutrigine pushes the key regulatory catalyst to year-end, though the absence of safety or manufacturing concerns and no new-study requests limits the downside to a procedural timeline shift.

items.name: The Middleby Corporation
items.headline: Midera secures $1B credit facility ahead of July 6 spin-off

items.name: eBay Inc.

items.decisionTakeaway: GameStop has formally proposed acquiring eBay at $125/share in cash and stock while already holding direct ownership plus option-based economic exposure to over 43M eBay shares, making this a high-stakes, unsolicited takeover attempt whose outcome hinges on eBay's board response and GameStop's ability to finance and obtain approvals.

items.name: GameStop Corp.

Company issued a press release on June 29, 2026 announcing the modification
items.decisionTakeaway: The $52.7M option-year exercise under an existing 10-year ASPR contract confirms continued U.S. government demand for ACAM2000 and adds near-term smallpox MCM revenue visibility for Emergent.

items.name: Marathon Petroleum Corporation
items.headline: MPC director Abdulaziz F. Alkhayyal passes away

No immediate announcement regarding replacement or board composition changes

items.name: Copart, Inc.
items.headline: Copart CEO Liaw steps down; former CEO Adair returns
whatChanged: Jeff Liaw stepping down as CEO and director effective July 31, 2026
Executive Chairman Jay Adair — Copart's prior CEO — appointed to resume CEO role effective July 31, 2026
Liaw to remain as Special Advisor to Adair during transition
No reason for departure disclosed; press release language is cordial with mutual praise
items.decisionTakeaway: An orderly CEO handoff back to the company's prior leader removes near-term execution uncertainty but raises questions about why Liaw is exiting after reportedly strong operational results.

items.name: TD SYNNEX Corporation
items.headline: TD SYNNEX files prospectus supplement for shelf offering
whatChanged: Filed a prospectus supplement under existing automatic shelf S-3 (Reg. No. 333-278517) on June 29, 2026
8-K filed solely to attach legality opinion from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (Exhibit 5.1) regarding issuance and sale of securities
No new financial results, guidance, or capital-raise terms disclosed in this filing
items.decisionTakeaway: This is an administrative legal-opinion filing tied to a prospectus supplement under an existing shelf; it signals a potential securities issuance but discloses no deal size, pricing, or use of proceeds here.

items.name: Comcast Corporation
items.headline: Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into separate public company
whatChanged: Comcast announced a tax-free spin-off of NBCUniversal and Sky into an independent publicly traded company, expected to complete in approximately one year
Mike Cavanagh will become CEO of the new NBCUniversal; former CFO Michael Angelakis will return as CEO of Comcast, with Brian Roberts remaining actively involved in leadership of both
Comcast intends to retain up to a 19.9% ownership stake in NBCUniversal for up to one year post-spin, to be monetized in a tax-efficient manner
Both companies are intended to have investment grade balance sheets; NBCUniversal will adopt Comcast's dual-class share structure
Goldman Sachs, PJT Partners, and Davis Polk & Wardwell are advising on the transaction
items.decisionTakeaway: Comcast is splitting into two focused public companies — a connectivity/technology business and a global media/entertainment business — which could unlock standalone value but introduces execution risk over the ~one-year separation timeline.

keyMetrics.comcastRetainedStakeInNBCUniversal: up to 19.9% for up to one year post-spin

items.name: Fortune Brands Innovations, Inc.
items.headline: Fortune Brands appoints Jesse Singh as permanent CEO
whatChanged: Jesse Singh appointed CEO and to the Board effective June 29, 2026; he was previously CEO of AZEK Company (2016–2025), where he drove EBITDA margin expansion and shareholder value
Interim CEO David Barry transitions to EVP and Chief Operating Officer, working alongside Singh
Interim CFO Ashley George continues in role while CFO search remains ongoing
Non-Executive Chair Susan Kilsby will directly oversee Fiberon operations amid previously disclosed strategic review of that business

Inducement awards granted to Singh effective July 1, 2026: 850,000 performance-based RSUs and 300,000 service-based stock options, vesting over 3–4 years subject to performance and continued employment
items.decisionTakeaway: The appointment of a proven building-products CEO with a track record of margin expansion at AZEK signals a potential operational turnaround direction for Fortune Brands, ending the interim leadership period that began March 16.

items.name: The Macerich Company
items.headline: Macerich underwriters exercise full greenshoe on 16.1M-share forward offering
whatChanged: Underwriters exercised in full their 30-day option to purchase 2,100,000 additional shares at $23.12325 per share, on top of the 14,000,000 firm shares already closed on June 17, 2026.
Company entered into Additional Forward Sale Agreements dated June 23, 2026 with the Forward Purchasers covering the option shares; closing of the option occurred June 26, 2026.
Forward Sellers borrowed and sold 2,100,000 shares to the Underwriters in connection with the Additional Forward Sale Agreements.
Company expects to physically settle the Additional Forward Sale Agreements and receive proceeds no later than June 16, 2027, though it may elect cash or net-share settlement instead.
Net proceeds will be contributed to the Operating Partnership to fund future acquisition opportunities and for general corporate purposes.
items.decisionTakeaway: The full greenshoe exercise increases the total forward-sale offering to 16.1 million shares, locking in incremental equity capacity for acquisitions while deferring cash proceeds to physical settlement by mid-2027.

items.name: SandRidge Energy, Inc.
items.headline: SandRidge to acquire Cherokee Play assets for $65M cash
whatChanged: Definitive agreement to acquire producing assets and leasehold interests in the Cherokee Play (Mid-Continent) for $65M cash, plus up to $6M in potential WTI-linked earn-outs
Acquired assets add ~3.0 MBoed net production (~43% oil), ~7,000 net leasehold acres, interests in 21 wells, and 8 proven development locations offsetting existing operations
Transaction described as immediately accretive to production, EBITDA, and free cash flow; expected to increase liquids mix on a pro forma basis
May 1, 2026 effective date with anticipated Q3 2026 closing; funded entirely with cash on hand, retaining meaningful cash balance post-close
items.decisionTakeaway: SandRidge is deploying $65M of cash on a bolt-on Cherokee Play acquisition that adds oily production and drilling inventory while remaining accretive to key metrics and preserving liquidity for future capital returns.

keyMetrics.potentialEarnOut: up to $6 million based on future WTI prices
